note: SUGGESTED starter `theme` labels for red-team objections — the common families an idea breaks on (they mirror the adversarial lenses). Recommendations only, never enforced: reuse one when it fits, invent freely when it doesn't. brief_red_team also surfaces the project's OWN prior themes for reuse.

items.hint: Too expensive / wrong pricing model for the value this persona perceives.
No reason to believe the vendor or product — brand, security, references.
Missing evidence it works for MY case — case study, trial, benchmark.
Migration, retraining or lock-in makes leaving the current tool costly.
'Good enough today' wins — no urgency to change anything.
The problem is not real or not painful enough for this persona.
A concrete capability gap blocks adoption (e.g. SSO, an integration).
Onboarding / setup / learning effort outweighs the promised gain.
A policy, legal or regulatory constraint forbids or stalls adoption.
A failure mode or worst case that would cause regret or churn.
